question: John gets a bonus that's the same percentage every year.  Last year he made $100,000 and got a $10,000 bonus.  This year he makes $200,000.  How much will John make this year when adding both his total pay and bonus together?

trajectory.content: John gets a bonus that's the same percentage every year.  Last year he made $100,000 and got a $10,000 bonus.  This year he makes $200,000.  How much will John make this year when adding both his total pay and bonus together? Make sure to state your answer at the end of the response.

To determine how much John will make this year, we first need to find the percentage of the bonus he received last year.

Last year, John made $100,000 and received a $10,000 bonus. The percentage of the bonus can be calculated as follows:

\[
\text{Bonus Percentage} = \left( \frac{\text{Bonus}}{\text{Salary}} \right) \times 100 = \left( \frac{10,000}{100,000} \right) \times 100 = 15\%
\]

Now that we know John receives a 15% bonus, we can calculate his bonus for this year based on his current salary of $200,000.

\[
\text{Bonus for this year} = 15\% \text{ of } 200,000 = 0.15 \times 200,000 = 30,000
\]

Next, we add his salary and his bonus for this year to find his total earnings:

\[
\text{Total Earnings} = \text{Salary} + \text{Bonus} = 200,000 + 30,000 = 240,000
\]

Therefore, John's total pay this year, including his salary and bonus, will be **$240,000**.
